name: prime

relevance.justification: PRIME directly satisfies all three core pillars: (1) species-level microbiome — ASV abundance tables at phylum-to-species resolution generated via SILVA 138.2 and Greengenes2 2024.09 pipelines; (2) rich phenotypic data — 101 standardized phenotypic categories covering disease status and health conditions; (3) per-sample demographic metadata including age, sex/gender, BMI, and lifestyle factors across 53,449 samples from 111 curated studies. Ethnicity is not explicitly confirmed as a captured field — flagged as a risk, not a missing pillar. Database is actively maintained (Zenodo v1 deposited July 2025, GG2 2024.09 pipeline). Structured tabular format (CSV). Standard biomedical identifiers (NCBI Taxonomy, NCBI SRA accessions, SILVA/GG2 taxonomy).

novelty.justification: PRIME is not represented in any current BioThings API. It provides: (a) 53,449 standardized 16S samples with cross-study harmonized metadata — substantially larger and more curated than HumanMetagenomeDB (NEEDS_REVIEW, 6 relevance score); (b) species-level ASV tables at two reference taxonomies (SILVA + GG2) enabling identifier crosswalk; (c) 101 manually curated phenotypic categories with structured sample-level metadata enabling phenotype-microbiome association queries not possible with raw SRA data. No overlap with existing BioThings gene/disease/variant/chemical APIs — entirely new entity type (microbiome community profiles linked to phenotype/demographics).

openness.justification: Zenodo deposit (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.15711237, v1, July 2025) is CC BY 4.0 + MIT licensed. All 28 files (9.1 GB) downloadable without login. Includes samples_metadata.csv (38.2 MB), projects_metadata.csv (108 kB), and 24 CSV taxonomic abundance tables. PRIME web interface (primedb.sjtu.edu.cn) and REST API (v1) also require no authentication. R package (primeDB) is MIT licensed. Minor risk: CC-BY requires attribution in derived products. Score 4 (not 5) due to CC-BY attribution requirement vs CC0.

evidence.scope: 53,449 human-associated 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing samples from 111 manually curated public studies. Covers 93 body sites organized into 10 high-level body systems (digestive/gut, oral, skin, respiratory, reproductive, mother-infant, cardiovascular, nervous, urinary, visual). Per-sample metadata: age, sex, BMI, lifestyle factors, disease status, body site, sequencing protocol. Taxonomic abundance tables at phylum-to-species level via SILVA 138.2 and Greengenes2 2024.09. 101 standardized phenotypic categories.
evidence.overlap: No direct overlap with existing BioThings APIs (MyChem, MyGene, MyDisease, MyVariant). HumanMetagenomeDB (currently NEEDS_REVIEW) covers WGS metagenomics with less metadata standardization. GMrepo focuses on gut microbiome disease associations from literature — PRIME is broader (all body sites) with richer per-sample demographics and standardized cross-study abundance tables.

urls.download_note: primedb.sjtu.edu.cn is JS-rendered — WebFetch returns only the page title with no extractable content from any subpath including /download. Zenodo is used as the bulk download source because the paper explicitly states data is 'permanently archived at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15711237' (canonical per the authors, not a stale mirror). Live site download availability should be manually verified during site inspection.

api_info.rate_limits: No enforced rate limiting; recommended max 10 req/sec
api_info.record_coverage: Full records — metadata, abundance tables, project info

risks: Ethnicity field not explicitly confirmed in sample-level metadata — verify in samples_metadata.csv before ingestion
Zenodo deposit (v1, July 2025) may lag behind live site updates; monitor for v2
16S rRNA only — no WGS/shotgun metagenomics; complementary to WGS-focused sources (HumanMetagenomeDB)
CC-BY 4.0 requires attribution in any derivative product or API response
Custom abundance generation on web interface takes up to 24h — bulk Zenodo download is the preferred ingestion path

primedb.sjtu.edu.cn is JS-rendered; canonical site download page could not be scraped — site inspection must manually verify whether a direct bulk download exists on the live site that supersedes the Zenodo deposit
next_actions: Run site inspection: verify samples_metadata.csv columns for ethnicity/race field
Confirm gut/digestive-system subset size (relevant to demographic-microbiome KG focus)
Proceed to plugin generation using Zenodo CSV files as canonical bulk download
